title: Recent social stress and severity of auditory hallucinations.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND AND HYPOTHESIS: Hearing voices is a common and often distressing experience for people with psychosis, and many individuals experience medication-resistant auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH). Psychosocial interventions are often employed to address distress over hearing voices. However, although links have been made between adverse social experiences and psychosis broadly, no work has yet delineated the relationship between day-to-day social stress and hallucination severity. We aim

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.
